items.title: Mamdani urges residents to stay indoors as blizzard conditions threaten East Coast
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Blizzard warnings were issued for New York City, New Jersey and coastal communities along the East Coast as a late-winter storm set to arrive on Sunday threatened to make a mess of the start of a new week.

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

items.title: Muslims in Milan break fast as Ramadan coincides with Winter Olympics
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>As Milan hosts events for the 2026 Winter Olympics, the city’s Muslim community is also observing the holy month of Ramadan, which has coincided as games are underway. At sunset each evening, worshippers gather at Al-Wahid mosque to break their fast and attend prayers.

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.title: Israeli airstrikes in eastern Lebanon kill 10, including Hezbollah members
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Israeli airstrikes on eastern Lebanon have killed eight members of the militant Hezbollah group, including several local officials, two officials with the group said Saturday. The Lebanese Health Ministry put the death toll at 10, but did not distinguish between militants and civilians.


Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.title: Ukraine strikes key industrial site deep inside Russia
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Ukrainian forces struck an industrial site deep inside Russia on Saturday, which Ukraine and unofficial Russian news channels say was a key state-owned missile factory. The attack in Russia’s Udmurt Republic left 11 people wounded, three of whom were hospitalized, according to a Telegram post by Sergei Bagin, the local health minister. Hours later, Ukraine's General Staff said its forces struck a key missile plant near the city of Votkinsk, using Ukrainian-made FP-5 “Flamingo” cruise missiles rather than drones.

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.title: Eleven injured in Ukrainian drone attack on central Russia
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>A Russian independent news channel, citing eyewitness footage, identified the site as a key state-owned missile plant. Three people were hospitalised, according to a Telegram post by Sergei Bagin - the local health minister for Russia’s Udmurt Republic, the region on the Volga river where the strike took place.


Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.title: Royal scandals over the last century
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>The arrest of Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor, formally known as Prince Andrew, is one of the gravest crises to strike the House of Windsor in more than a century. But it's just the latest in a series of shocks for the British monarchy, which has endured abdication, scandal and waves of public backlash. AP's Luke Garratt looks at the moments that have tested the monarchy’s survival in the modern times.

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.title: Business owners react to Supreme Court striking down Trump's tariffs
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>The U.S. Supreme Court has struck down some of President Donald Trump’s most sweeping tariffs. But small business owners are worried things are far from over.

items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>The U.S. military said Friday that it has carried out another deadly strike on a vessel accused of trafficking drugs in the Eastern Pacific Ocean.

items.title: Legal expert answers what happens next after former Prince Andrew's arrest
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Legal expert Joshua Rozenberg said prosecuting Mountbatten-Windsor, who was arrested and released Thursday, would mean proving that he was acting in a "public office" whilst he was trade envoy and that he "wilfully misconducted himself" leading to "abuse of the public's trust."

items.title: Melania Trump donates inaugural ball gown to Smithsonian National Museum
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Melania Trump is donating her 2025 inaugural ball gown to the Smithsonian National Museum of American History’s First Ladies Collection, a rare distinction held by only a few first ladies in history.

items.title: Fatal drone attack in Ukrainian city of Kharkiv
items.author: Associated Press
items.content: <article>At least one person has died as a result of a drone attack in Kharkiv, Ukraine's State Emergency Service said Friday.</article>

items.title: How to safely dispose of old household batteries
items.author: Associated Press
items.content: <article>Safely disposing of household batteries is good for the home and good for the environment. Dead batteries can leak heavy metals into soil and water once they reach the landfill. They can also overheat and cause fires in garbage trucks and recycling centers. Here's what you can do.</article>

items.title: Aid cuts force World Food Programme to turn away 3 in 4 children in Afghanistan
items.author: Associated Press
items.content: <article>The Taliban takeover in 2021 saw direct foreign aid halted almost overnight, driving millions into poverty and hunger. Now funding cuts to humanitarian organizations — including the halting of U.S. aid to programs such as the WFP's food distribution — have severed a lifeline for millions.</article>

items.title: Members of Congress react to Supreme Court striking down Trump's tariffs
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>The Supreme Court struck down some of President Donald Trump's most sweeping tariffs on Friday, in a 6-3 decision that he overstepped his authority when using an emergency powers law to justify new taxes on goods from nearly every country in the world. (AP Production: Marissa Duhaney)

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.title: Trump directs the Pentagon to release UFO files: 'Tremendous interest'
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>President Donald Trump says he’s directing the Pentagon and other government agencies to identify and release the files, after former President Barack Obama said he thought the "odds are good" that other life exists in the vast universe.
